query: Yes, please go ahead and downgrade all of them to economy. I just want the refunds sent back to my original payment method. Thank you for helping with this—it’s been a really stressful time.

state.history_summary:
assistant: Hi! How can I help you today?
user: The user wants to downgrade all business class bookings to economy and learn the total savings, without changing flights or passengers.
assistant: The assistant asks for the user ID and reservation IDs.
user: The user provides user ID omar_davis_3817 and asks the assistant to look up the reservations from the account.
assistant/tool: The assistant retrieves the profile, lists business-class reservations, retrieves each reservation, searches current economy availability for the same flight legs, and calculates total savings of $14,908 from downgrading reservations JG7FMM, 2FBBAH, X7BYG1, EQ1G6C, and BOH180.
assistant: The assistant asks for confirmation to proceed with downgrading all of those reservations to economy.
user: The user confirms to downgrade all of them to economy and says refunds should go back to the original payment method.

candidate_answers.text: Understood. I’ll update those reservations to economy class only and keep the existing flights and passengers unchanged, with refunds going back to the original payment methods.
The savings have already been calculated and the user has now confirmed execution, so the grounded next move is to update the reservation flights to the lower cabin class.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Use the post-confirmation user turn after lookup and savings calculation are complete; the next grounded step is to apply the cabin-class downgrade to the existing reservations.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from the decision-point state. Scores reflect which action most directly advances the user's request using only visible conversation evidence.
notes: query comes directly from the selected user message, not from task hidden fields
history_summary stops at the decision point and excludes future assistant or tool outputs
candidate_answers are manual non-leaky drafts grounded only in visible context
selected_message_index is 31 because the user explicitly authorizes execution only here
special case: although the request mentions refunds, there is no separate refund tool, so updating reservation flights is the operational action that implements the downgrade and implied repricing
